questions.q: Angles in matching corners at the two intersections form which pair?

options.why: Uses opposite interior positions instead of matching corners.
Uses same-side interior positions instead of matching corners.
Uses opposite angles at one intersection rather than two intersections.

options.text: Interior and on opposite sides of the transversal
Interior and on the same side of the transversal
options.why: Uses the positional definition of co-interior angles.

options.text: Matching corners at the two intersections
options.why: Uses the positional definition of corresponding angles.

options.text: Opposite each other at one intersection
options.why: Uses the positional definition of vertically opposite angles.

options.text: Interior and on the same side of the transversal
Interior and on opposite sides of the transversal
options.why: Uses the positional definition of alternate angles.

options.text: Matching corners at the two intersections
options.why: Uses the positional definition of corresponding angles.
Looks at a single intersection instead of the interior strip.

questions.q: Two exterior angles lie on the same side of a transversal but not in matching corners. Which named pair are they?

options.why: Uses same-side position but ignores the requirement for matching corners.
Ignores that alternate angles must be interior and on opposite sides.
Ignores that co-interior angles must both lie between the lines.
